title: stack traces attached to history entries
manager: Record, on each history entry, the call-site that caused the transition, so a machine's history reads as a debuggable trail — you can see not just that it moved from A to B but which line of code drove it there. It turns the history log from a bare state list into a forensic record, which is exactly what you want the moment a machine does something surprising in production.
cs: stack capture is costly, so it is opt-in / dev-mode; ties into the richer error-object model (§11 last_error/stack) the era's error work builds.
